text: God, it would seem, gave Balaam express permission to go with the Moabites, meaning to shew his power by making the prophet bless Israel, instead of cursing them. But he was angry with Balaam for wanting to go (because it argued covetousness in him, II Pet. 2.15). These two attitudes are here represented as successive, as if Almighty God had changed his mind; such representation of divine things in human terms is common all through the Old Testament.
‘This city on the frontier of Balac’s dominions’; the words probably conceal a proper name, ‘City of the Outposts’.
